title: pilosella peleteriana subsp. peleteriana
intro: Pilosella peleteriana subsp. peleteriana, also known as the Alpine Mouse-ear or Rock Mouse-ear, is a rare and endangered flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with leaves that resemble a mouse's ear, which is why it is named as such. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. Pilosella peleteriana subsp. peleteriana has been traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions and tinctures, to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
